title: <div><tt>| B30  | granted   | denied    | access        | ritual verdict       |
content:
| B30 | granted | denied | access | ritual verdict |
| B31 | start | stop | control | execution flag |
| B32 | known | erased | memory | data presence |

---

## | PROTOCOL NOTE |

These binary pairs form the **hard backbone** of the narrative operating system.
While DUALCORE and HYBRID tables **breathe**, this M1RROR table **cuts**.
They are used to:

- Finalize outcomes
- End recursion
- Trigger irreversible changes
- Control synth module routing states
- Gatekeeper behavior in AI character logic

They **cannot be fused**.
They must be **respected or defied**.
This is the table of **absolutes**.

---
